thresholdSource: No lab panic value established for ferritin (no StatPearls/Tietz acute critical-value source found). plausibleHigh 100000: above a documented serum ferritin of 78,662 ng/mL in a case of adult-onset Still's disease with hemophagocytic syndrome, patient alive and transferred for further care (PMC5726146); values >100,000 ng/mL have also been reported in critically ill patients with hyperferritinemia (PMC8964169). Accuracy bound only, not a clinical-action threshold. No criticalLow/plausibleLow — low ferritin reflects depleted stores, not an acute emergency. Draft — not clinician-approved.
